title: 4-max UTG RFI
notes: 4-max UTG is the 3-behind seat (~6-max CO equivalent), so the open is wide (~24-25%). The 1bb ante improves steal pot odds and pushes inclusion of low suited gappers/one-gappers and partial offsuit broadways. callPct=0 everywhere (UTG opens first-in; no limp/cold-call option). All pairs open pure 44+; 33/22 mixed lower. Suited aces full to A2s (A2s mixed at the tail; wheel aces A5s-A4s pure for blocker/playability). Offsuit broadways: AJo+/ATo/KQo pure; A9o/KJo/KTo/QJo/QTo/JTo partial. Suited connectors run to 54s with 64s as a thin mixed bottom; one-gappers (T7s/86s/75s) and suited K-lows (K5s-K6s) enter as low-freq mixes. Approximate study-grade, not solver-exact.

title: 4-max BTN RFI
notes: 4-max BTN is a very wide steal spot. With only SB/BB behind and a 1bb ante sweetening the pot, BTN opens ~48% of hands. All pocket pairs, all suited aces (A2s+), all suited kings (K2s+), and all suited queens (Q2s+) are in (weakest with small mixing). Offsuit aces run all the way to A2o, offsuit kings to K6o, offsuit queens to Q7o. Suited connectors/gappers extend to 32s/43s/53s/64s/74s region. Marginal cutoffs use mixed frequencies. callPct=0 since BTN is first-in facing only UTG's fold; all action is open or fold. Sizing 2.2bb is standard for ~40bb ante games. Study approximations aligned to published solver charts, not exact solver outputs.

title: 4-max SB RFI (raise-or-fold)
notes: 4-max SB RFI is heads-up vs BB only, so the range is very wide (~47%), wider than a 6-max SB open. The 1bb ante improves steal pot odds, pushing borderline offsuit broadways/aces and weak suited hands into the open. All pocket pairs, all suited aces, and effectively all suited kings down through K5s are pure opens. Marginal offsuit hands (low Ax, Kx, Qx, Jx) and the weakest suited gappers are mixed at the bottom. callPct=0 by construction (raise-or-fold). Full GTO solutions include a mixed-limp strategy for some marginal/trap hands at this depth; here it is collapsed into raise-or-fold. Sizing 3bb is standard; some solvers prefer a smaller 2-2.5x SB-vs-BB open, but 3bb is used per spec. Study-grade approximations aligned with published charts, not exact solver outputs.

title: 4-max BB vs UTG open
sizing: 3bet to ~9bb (linear/polar mix; OOP so slightly larger)
rangePct: ~9% 3bet on top of a very wide flat-call defense (overall defend ~55-60% of hands; only ~21% combo-weighted shown at material frequency)
notes: BB closes the action with 1bb already invested plus the 1bb ante in the pot, so it is getting an excellent price and must defend VERY wide vs a single tight UTG open - overwhelmingly by flat-calling. The default is call: all pairs (22+), every suited ace, suited broadways, suited connectors/one-gappers down to ~54s, and the strong offsuit broadways. The 3bet (to ~9bb, slightly larger because OOP) is the smaller polarized branch: value = QQ+, AKs, AKo, plus mixed JJ/AQs/AJs/KQs; bluffs = wheel suited aces (A5s/A4s primary, A3s/A2s lighter) which block UTG's AA/AKo while keeping nut-flush + wheel-straight backup, plus a touch of 76s/54s. Because UTG is the tightest opener in 4-max, the 3bet stays value-weighted and the wheel-Ax bluffs prefer 3bet-or-call over fold. Frequencies are mixed/approximate, study-grade, not exact solver output.

title: 4-max BB vs BTN open
sizing: 3bet to ~9-9.5bb (linear-leaning polar; BB OOP so slightly larger)
rangePct: ~10-12% 3bet (polar); plus very wide flat-call. Total BB defense ~50-55% of all hands (ante + closing action = huge pot odds, fold very little)
notes: Approximate study-grade chart, NOT solver-exact. 4-max BB vs BTN open at ~40bb with 1bb ante. BB closes the action and gets ~3:1+ pot odds with the ante, so it defends very wide: total defense is ~50-55% of all hands and the bottom of the range is mostly flat-call, not fold. 3bet is polar/blocker-heavy to ~9-9.5bb at ~10-12% overall: value (QQ+, AK, AQs, KQs, AJs/AQo as mixed) plus blocker bluffs (suited wheel aces A5s-A2s, A5o/A4o, K-blockers, suited connectors/one-gappers). Suited wheel aces (A5s strongest) are the preferred bluffs for the nut-flush blocker. Calls dominate; bluff-3bet frequencies are intentionally low-mix.

title: 4-max BB vs SB open
sizing: 3bet to ~8bb (roughly 3.2x a 2.5bb SB open); polar value + blocker bluffs
rangePct: ~15-16% 3bet, ~68% total defense (3bet + call). Folds only the deepest offsuit/weak-suited junk.
notes: HU BB defense vs SB open, BB IP. SB steals very wide (~45-55%), BB closes the action IP with the 1bb ante sweetening the price, so BB defends extremely wide (~68%), folding only the deepest offsuit junk and the worst weak-suited holdings. 3bet (~15-16%) is polar: linear value (TT+, AQ+, KQs, AJs) plus blocker bluffs (wheel aces A2s-A5s 3bet at elevated freq, suited Kx blockers, low suited connectors). Most playable hands prefer the flat (call) given position and a discounted price, so call frequencies dominate across broadways, suited gappers, and small pairs. Sizing ~8bb keeps a healthy value/bluff ratio. Adjust vs a tighter or limp-heavy SB by trimming the thinnest offsuit calls and merging the 3bet toward value.

title: 4-max BTN vs UTG open
notes: 4-max BTN faces a single UTG opener with SB+BB still behind, so this is an IP cold-3bet/flat/fold node. Value 3bets: QQ+/AKs/AKo at high frequency, JJ/AQs mixed. Bluff 3bets are wheel suited aces (A5s/A4s/A3s, some A2s) that block AA/AKs/AKo and retain nut-flush/wheel playability. Suited broadways (KQs/KJs/QJs/JTs) and suited connectors mostly flat IP, taking a 3bet only at low frequency for balance. Pairs 22-JJ and offsuit broadways (AQo/AJo/KQo) prefer flatting to realize equity in position vs UTG's tight range. The 1bb ante sweetens pot odds and widens the flat/defend tail (A2s, 54s). Net cold-3bet ~5.7% with ~48% pure value (slightly bluff-leaning, correct for IP). Sized ~7bb to deny the two cold-callers behind and the BB closing. Reduce bluffs and lean toward flatting vs sticky SB/BB.

title: 4-max SB vs UTG open
notes: 4-max SB vs UTG open, OOP with BB still behind => pure 3bet-or-fold polar strategy at ~11%, essentially no flatting (dominated/squeezed OOP). Value core: TT+, AQs+, AKo, AQo, plus KQs/AJs at high freq. Bluff region is suited wheel aces (A5s-A2s) for blocker value plus the best suited Broadways/connectors (KJs, KTs, QJs, JTs, suited connectors) that flop well and unblock folds. Wheel-ace ladder runs A5s>A4s>A3s>A2s (best blocker on top). Mid/low pairs form a clean descending bluff ladder TT>99>88>77>66>55>44. The 1bb ante sweetens pot odds so upper bluffs run slightly hotter than a 6-max equivalent, but UTG's tight opening range keeps the spot disciplined - wider Ax offsuit and weak Kx offsuit (only K9s as a thin blocker bluff) stay folds. Sizing ~9bb (slightly larger OOP) to deny BB cheap overcalls and charge UTG's flats.

title: 4-max SB vs BTN open
notes: SB plays pure 3bet-or-fold OOP vs a wide BTN open; the 1bb ante boosts the incentive to attack so the range is ~15% and polar (no flatting, callPct=0 everywhere). Linear value (AA-TT, AK-AQ, AJs, KQs, broadway suited) plus blocker-driven bluffs: wheel/nut-blocker aces A5s-A2s preferred, low suited broadways/Kx as backup-equity bluffs, suited connectors and one-gappers as folds-to-4bet semibluffs. Offsuit bluffs kept minimal and blocker-based (AQo/KQo/AJo at value-ish freq; A9o/A5o/A4o thin wheel/Ax-blocker bluffs). Pocket pairs below TT 3bet at a decreasing taper. 4bet-continuing range vs a BTN jam/4bet is roughly QQ+/AKs at 40bb.

title: 4-max BTN open vs SB 3bet
rangePct: ~8% 4bet of BTN's opening range (value + A5s/A4s wheel bluffs); plus a flat-call tier 88-JJ/AQs/AJs/ATs/KQs/KJs
notes: Approximate study-grade, NOT solver-exact. At 40bb in position BTN plays a polarized 4bet-or-call structure vs the SB's OOP 3bet. Pure value 4bets: AA/KK, AKs, AKo (mostly). Near-pure value mix: QQ. Polar bluff 4bets come ONLY from the A5s/A4s wheel blockers (best card-removal vs SB's Ax value and AA/QQ, with a suited backup if called). JJ-down, AQs/AJs/ATs, KQs/KJs prefer flatting in position to realize equity and keep SB's 3bet bluffs in. Hands marked aggroPct (label "4bet") = the 4bet portion; callPct = flat-call the 3bet; rest fold. Total 4bet frequency lands near ~8% of BTN's opening range. The 1bb ante and 4-max dynamics widen SB's 3bet, justifying a slightly fatter bluff allocation and wider IP calls than 6-max, but BTN does NOT continue with dominated trash. ~18bb 4bet sizing prices SB into a ~22bb jam-or-fold while committing roughly 45% of effective stacks.
